# Letta Field Guide

> A concept-level field guide to the Letta v2 agent harness, written by Doc Holiday
> (https://doc.holiday) by exploring the letta-code and letta-agent-sdk sources directly.
> Each page explains why a subsystem exists, its central abstractions, and how it relates
> to its neighbors. Every doc page is also served as plain markdown at <page-url>/index.md.

## Pages

- [Anatomy of a Turn](/docs/anatomy-of-a-turn/): one turn traced end to end, from message to streamed reply
- [Dreaming and Reflection](/docs/dreaming-and-reflection/): sleep-time compute — how agents rewrite their own memory in the background
- [Skills, Subagents, and Mods](/docs/skills-subagents-and-mods/): the three extension mechanisms and why each exists
- [Tools, Permissions, and Sandboxing](/docs/tools-permissions-and-sandboxing/): client-side tool execution and the gates around it
- [Channels](/docs/channels/): one agent on many surfaces — Slack, Telegram, Discord, and custom
- [The App Server and the SDK](/docs/the-app-server-and-the-sdk/): the websocket protocol seam and embedding agents programmatically
- [About This Site](/docs/about-this-site/): scope, audience, provenance

## See also

- Official Letta documentation: https://docs.letta.com/letta-agent
- letta-code source: https://github.com/letta-ai/letta-code
- letta-agent-sdk source: https://github.com/letta-ai/letta-agent-sdk
